academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year bachelor's degree or equivalent and must demonstrate prior preparation in political science, international relations, sociology, and international law.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor diploma (or equivalent), with a minimum three-year undergraduate degree.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must have proven skills in Political Sciences, International Relations, Sociology, and International Law.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Country-based specific entry title and GPA requirements apply through the University of Padua central rules. The current 2026/2027 live apply page does not publish a programme-specific GPA threshold [uncertain].

accepted_proofs: TOEFL iBT with a minimum score of 80 for B2.
IELTS Academic or General Training with a minimum score of 6.0 for B2.
Cambridge English B2 First with a minimum score of 173.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English with a minimum score of 126 for B2.
Pearson English International Certificate Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic with a minimum score of 65 for B2.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if their entry qualification for admission was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries recognised by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: As of 3 June 2026, the live 2026/2027 apply page shows only one call. Older cached official snippets still display the generic three-call wording used on other open-access programmes [uncertain].

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: As of 3 June 2026, the live 2026/2027 apply page shows only one call and does not separate applicant categories on-page [uncertain].

required_documents.notes: Required for English-taught master's degree applications.
Required for English-taught master's degree applications.
If the transcript is not issued in Italian or English, an official translation must be uploaded at application stage.
If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted. No legalised documents are required at application stage.
EU or equivalent applicants may use an ID card; other applicants use a passport.
Required only for non-EU citizens already residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or accepted exemption proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption based on previous education entirely in English or native-speaker status.

required_documents.document_name: CIMEA Statement of Comparability of the Bachelor's degree
required_documents.notes: The Admissions and Welcome Unit may request it if further verification of the qualification is needed.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: A post-secondary qualification awarded by a non-university higher education institution is also accepted if it allows admission to master's degrees.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: The transcript should state passed exams, grades, and preferably ECTS credits and programme description.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and Study Visa D - enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through Universitaly no later than 15 July and need a study visa to enter Italy.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt (Assicurata)
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants; after arrival in Italy, the residence permit application must be filed within 8 working days.

entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The current 2026/2027 live programme and apply pages state admission requirements based on prior degree, subject preparation, country-based title and GPA rules, and English proficiency, but no programme-specific entry test or interview.

source_quotes.quote: Access Open access with admission requirements
proven skills in Political Sciences, International Relations, Sociology and International Law
You obtained your entry title by completing your previous education entirely in English
native English speaker coming from one of the following countries

uncertainty_notes: The live 2026/2027 apply page on 3 June 2026 shows only one application call, while some older cached official snippets still show the standard three-call text used for other open-access programmes.
The live 2026/2027 course page routes applicants to a central country-based entry-title and GPA table, but it does not publish a programme-specific GPA threshold.
The English-language deadline is taken from the central 2026/2027 language-requirement page rule for open-access programmes rather than from a programme-specific admission notice.
